summary: Implementation successfully delivers in-app notifications with staggered delivery and signal feed pagination. The notifications table is properly designed with deliver_at for staggered timing. Premium users get immediate delivery, free users get 15-minute delay. The notification bell UI with badge works correctly, dropdown shows notifications with proper styling, and clicking marks as read. Feed pagination with 'Load more' button is implemented. All TypeScript checks pass.

issues.description: The unused DBNotification, markNotificationRead, and getUnreadNotificationCount imports in notifications/route.ts should be cleaned up to reduce lint warnings.

sharedStateObservations.suggestion: Document the staggered delivery pattern in architecture.md. The deliver_at timestamp approach allows for time-based feature gating without requiring background jobs. This pattern could be reused for other delayed delivery scenarios.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: notifications table schema in db.ts line 328 shows deliver_at TIMESTAMPTZ. trigger/route.ts lines 145-150 show the tier-based delivery time calculation (now vs now + 15min).

sharedStateObservations.suggestion: Add the notification polling interval (30 seconds) to environment.md or architecture.md as a configurable constant. Currently hardcoded in Nav component.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Nav.tsx line 95 shows setInterval for polling every 30000ms.

sharedStateObservations.suggestion: Document the user_settings table schema and its role in notification preferences and risk tolerance. Currently mentioned in db.ts but not in architecture documentation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: db.ts lines 334-342 show user_settings CREATE TABLE with risk_tolerance and notification preferences (notify_in_app, notify_email, notify_phone).

codeReview.summary: Excellent implementation with clean database design and proper API structure. The staggered delivery logic is elegantly simple using deliver_at timestamp comparison. The notification UI is well-integrated with the existing Nav component. Feed pagination uses standard offset/limit pattern. All routes properly check authentication. The mark-all-as-read utility function is a nice touch for UX.
